title: 04 car outgrows neighborhood
prompt: Use case: ads-marketing. Image 1 controls Tina, wardrobe, product, and dark car design. Image 2 supports identity and candy only. Image 3 supplies the peelable-car concept, but reject its orange upholstery, parachute, camera, and disconnected parts. Create a bright-daylight wide exterior scale tableau. Tina's dark car has grown to exactly twice the height of the one-story suburban houses around it. Its smooth roof skin peels open in two broad candy-colored curls with pearly pink inner surfaces. Tina remains located at the enlarged back-seat opening and rises only enough to look out, still holding the pouch. Tiny neighbors watch from lawns to establish scale. Make the car enormous but structurally coherent. No monster vehicle, crushed buildings, disaster, giant Tina, or ordinary full-size houses.

resolved_params.system_prompt: Create one polished cinematic live-action Peelerz commercial styleframe, never a collage or storyboard grid. Follow the stated role of every reference image rather than blending all references indiscriminately. Preserve Tina as the same bookish eighteen-year-old young woman with long brown hair, blue jacket, black top, pink lychee gummy, and pink Peelerz pouch. Tina's fixed meeting point is the back seat of the car: never move her into the driver or front passenger seat. Make the specified physical situation immediately legible in one 16:9 image. Keep transformations benign, youthful, tactile, and suitable for a live-action candy commercial. Avoid captions, borders, split screens, watermarks, generic candyland, random rainbow effects, body horror, violent wreckage, and added package copy.
